summary: Iteration 3 backend testing complete. All 33 pytest tests PASSED (100%, 102s). Added 12 new TestAnalytics tests covering POST /api/analytics/event, GET /api/analytics/summary?days=N, and auto-event recording across translate/* + dictionary search. Verified empty-state safety (DELETE /history clears db.translations -> totals.translations=0, by_mode/by_language/top_words=[]; events DO persist independently). Verified that a unique event type pushed via POST /analytics/event appears in by_type, that text_to_sign/translate_frames/fingerspelling/dictionary_search counters increment by exactly 1 per call, that GET /dictionary without q does NOT record an event, that the by_day series length matches the days query param (3, 14, 30), and that 422 is returned for malformed event payloads. All iteration 1+2 regression tests still pass: health, dictionary (68 entries) + filters/languages, text-to-sign, video, history CRUD, frames, fingerspelling, get-by-id.

minor.issue: record_event silently swallows ALL exceptions (try/except Exception: pass). If db.events insertion is broken, /analytics/event still returns {ok:true} - violates honest API contract. Consider logging the exception and/or returning a soft error flag while still not breaking caller flows.

No payload size / depth limits on `data` (free-form dict). A client can submit very large nested objects that bloat the events collection. Consider enforcing a max-size or whitelist of keys per event type.
No event-type validation (any string accepted). Typos and adversarial values pollute by_type aggregations. Consider an enum / regex (e.g., ^[a-z_]{1,40}$).
`days` query param is unbounded; days=1000000 will still build a 1M-element by_day list and may OOM. Add Query(..., ge=1, le=365).

Iterates db.translations.find(...).to_list(5000) in-memory and word-counts in Python; will not scale past ~thousands of translations. Move word frequency + by_day grouping into a Mongo aggregation pipeline (already used for by_type and dict_top).

by_day grouping relies on `created_at` being stored as ISO string (substring [:10]). If a future code path stores datetime, the bucket silently disappears. Add isinstance handling for datetime as well, or normalize on insert.
On Gemini failure (HTTPException 502) the endpoint never calls record_event. Consider recording a 'translate_video_failed' event for ops visibility (same applies to /frames and /fingerspelling).

record_event is awaited inline before returning -- adds latency to every search. Consider asyncio.create_task(record_event(...)) (fire-and-forget) so dictionary search latency does not depend on Mongo write.
Unchanged from iteration 1/2: returns 200 with deleted:0 instead of 404 when id does not exist (carried over).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: record_event swallows all exceptions silently. At minimum log the failure (logger.warning) so missing events are observable in production.

server.py: Order of declaration matters - _STOPWORDS is referenced inside analytics_summary (line ~202) but defined AFTER the function (line ~241). Works because the lookup happens at call-time, but it's fragile. Move _STOPWORDS above analytics_summary.
server.py: AnalyticsEvent.data is `Optional[dict]` with no validation. Add a max-key-count or allowed-types restriction to harden the public POST endpoint against abuse.
server.py: /api/analytics/summary `days: int = 14` parameter is unbounded. Use `days: int = Query(14, ge=1, le=365)` to prevent abuse.
server.py: top_dictionary_searches groups by raw `data.q` value (no normalization). 'Hola' and 'hola' will be different buckets. Lowercase/strip the q in record_event or in the aggregation.

server.py: Word frequency tokenizer (line 200-203) splits on whitespace then strips non-alpha; combined characters with accents like 'á' are kept (good), but punctuation-attached tokens like 'hola,cómo' (no space) collapse to 'holacomo'. Use re.findall(r'\\b[\\wáéíóúüñ]+\\b', text, re.IGNORECASE) for cleaner tokenization.
server.py: SEED_DICTIONARY (~75 inline entries) should be moved to backend/data/dictionary.json -- carried over from iteration 2.
server.py: Still no rate limiting on Gemini-backed endpoints; analytics endpoints are unauthenticated and can be flooded to inflate by_type counters.
server.py: /api/analytics/event accepts arbitrary unauthenticated input. If exposed publicly, a script can flood the DB. Recommend at minimum a per-IP rate limit (slowapi).
seed_data_creation: Tests create unique TEST_*_{uuid8} event types via POST /analytics/event so before/after by_type deltas are deterministic. Dictionary searches use unique TEST_q_{uuid6} queries so top_dictionary_searches assertions are unambiguous. test_summary_empty_state_is_safe and test_events_persist_after_history_clear call DELETE /api/history which clears db.translations only (not db.events) -- this is a documented invariant in the iteration 3 spec.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now contain 33 tests across 9 classes (~102s wall-clock with real Gemini calls). New TestAnalytics class (12 tests) covers iteration 3. AI endpoints (/translate/video, /frames, /fingerspelling) accept either 200 or 502 from Gemini; analytics tests for those endpoints branch on status (event recorded only when status=200). DELETE /history clears db.translations but NOT db.events; tests rely on this invariant. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://hands-face-translate.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Iteration 3 analytics endpoints behave per spec; auto-event recording in translate/* and dictionary search verified end-to-end via summary aggregation.
